StabilityRunbook.Action: Export Stability Workbench and build a support package immediately after a crash.
StabilityRunbook.Why: Target=forzahorizon6.exe; timeline rows=130; top score=Memory/runtime fault
StabilityRunbook.SuccessCheck: A new export/support zip exists after the latest crash timestamp.

StabilityRunbook.Action: Stop overlay, capture, tuning, input-hook, and performance-monitor tools for one launch.
StabilityRunbook.SuccessCheck: The same crash fingerprint disappears, changes module/code, or still reproduces without those processes.

StabilityRunbook.Action: Collect DxDiag/WPR evidence, clean-install the GPU driver if needed, and test without capture/HAGS-style variables.
StabilityRunbook.Why: 11 GPU/TDR timeline row(s); 11 GPU/System display event(s) in 30 days
StabilityRunbook.SuccessCheck: GPU/TDR rows stop appearing near the app crash, or the crash code/module changes.

StabilityRunbook.Action: Repair Visual C++/.NET/DirectX/media prerequisites and keep a before/after fingerprint comparison.
StabilityRunbook.Why: 16x Access violation c0000005 module=StackHash_2264; 8x Access violation 0xc0000005 module=unknown
StabilityRunbook.SuccessCheck: The top access-violation/dependency fingerprint stops repeating after repair.

StabilityRunbook.Action: Compare crashes against recent driver/update/software changes and isolate or roll back one suspect at a time.
StabilityRunbook.Why: 62 change row(s) near the crash anchor.
StabilityRunbook.SuccessCheck: A single isolated change alters or removes the crash fingerprint.

StabilityRunbook.Action: With Steam Cloud off, use Deep Fresh for save/cache/settings user-data only, then launch once.
StabilityRunbook.Why: FH6 target detected; this is useful only after evidence is captured and backups exist.
StabilityRunbook.SuccessCheck: New local state is created and the crash either clears or repeats with the same fingerprint.

StabilityRunbook.Action: If the fingerprint survives clean tests, export CrashScope, Stability, support package, DxDiag, and dump evidence for vendor/support escalation.
StabilityRunbook.Why: Stable fingerprints after controlled tests are stronger than repeated blind cleanup.
StabilityRunbook.SuccessCheck: Package contains timeline, scores, WER, Reliability, GPU, security, and dump/config evidence.

Use the same launch path and compare whether the top fingerprint changes.
Export support package before WER evidence ages out.
Use generated LocalDumps/ProcDump commands only when stronger evidence is needed.

ExperimentQuality.Evidence: No support package newer than the latest crash anchor.
ExperimentQuality.NextAction: Build Support Zip immediately after the next crash.

ExperimentPlan.Action: Export Stability Workbench, run Support Zip, and note the exact crash fingerprint before changing anything.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Same target, same launch path, same graphics settings, same account.
ExperimentPlan.SuccessCheck: Baseline has crash event, fingerprint, timeline, and package artifacts.

ExperimentPlan.Action: Launch once with no new fixes to confirm the crash still reproduces.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Do not change drivers, overlays, saves, config, or launch options between baseline and control.
ExperimentPlan.SuccessCheck: Same fingerprint repeats, or the app stays stable and journal records that surprise result.

ExperimentPlan.Action: Stop overlay, capture, tuning, input-hook, and performance-monitor tools for exactly one test.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Leave save data, driver, Windows settings, and launch path unchanged.
ExperimentPlan.SuccessCheck: Fingerprint disappears or changes when overlay/capture stack is absent.

ExperimentPlan.Action: Test a clean graphics path: no capture overlay, stable power mode, then clean-install/update GPU driver only if evidence still points there.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Do not wipe saves or change runtimes during the GPU-path test.
ExperimentPlan.SuccessCheck: GPU/TDR rows stop appearing near crashes, or crash code/module changes.

ExperimentPlan.Action: Repair VC++/.NET/DirectX/media prerequisites, then run one launch with all other variables frozen.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Do not change GPU driver, saves, or overlays in the same attempt.
ExperimentPlan.Evidence: 16x Access violation c0000005 module=StackHash_2264; 8x Access violation 0xc0000005 module=unknown

ExperimentPlan.Action: With Steam Cloud off and backups present, run Deep Fresh for local save/cache/settings only.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Keep overlays, driver, runtimes, and launch path unchanged for this test.
ExperimentPlan.SuccessCheck: Fresh local data is recreated and the crash either clears or repeats with the same fingerprint.

ExperimentPlan.Action: Use a Microsoft clean-boot style isolation only after simpler one-variable tests are logged.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Re-enable services/startup items in groups so the journal can identify the culprit.
ExperimentPlan.SuccessCheck: Crash reproduces only when a specific service/startup group returns, or remains stable in clean boot.
ExperimentPlan.Evidence: Used when no visible conflict process explains the crash.

ExperimentPlan.Action: If the same fingerprint survives controlled tests, collect dump/WPR/ProcMon-style evidence and export a full package.
ExperimentPlan.KeepConstant: Keep the repro short and targeted; do not leave heavy tracing enabled.
ExperimentPlan.SuccessCheck: Support package contains dump/config/timeline/runbook/journal evidence tied to one fingerprint.
